description_long: This is a small, vividly colored crustacean with a robust, glossy exoskeleton. Its most striking feature is a pair of large, asymmetrical claws, with one claw significantly larger and more prominent than the other, both displaying a gradient of orange, white, and brown hues. The body is compact and segmented, with a dark brown cephalothorax and a slightly lighter abdomen. The legs are slender, jointed, and covered in fine setae, giving them a spiny appearance. The eyes are positioned on short stalks, providing a wide field of vision. The overall appearance suggests a fiddler crab, commonly found in coastal or urban park environments near water.
description: A small, glossy crustacean with one oversized orange-white claw, spiny legs, and stalked eyes. Its body is dark brown with orange highlights.
description_view_0: Front view showing both claws, with the larger claw raised and the spiny legs splayed outward.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the oversized claw and the segmentation of the body, with visible setae on the legs.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the back of the cephalothorax, both claws, and the arrangement of the legs.
description_view_3: Angled side view emphasizing the large claw and the curvature of the legs.
